title: Ensure predefined games (Match Word to Image) always appear on Home

requirements.text: Ensure the backend game catalog is automatically seeded with the predefined games at canister initialization and after upgrades so that at minimum the predefined game "Match Word to Image" (ID: "match-word-to-image") always exists and is returned by getAllGames().

quotes: it should show the predefined games i create here

acceptanceCriteria: On a fresh deploy with no prior state, calling getAllGames() returns an array that includes a game with id "match-word-to-image".
After an upgrade, if the game with id "match-word-to-image" was missing, it is re-added automatically without requiring any frontend action.
No user-facing text is introduced on the backend.

requirements.text: Fix the Home page end-to-end behavior so the predefined "Match Word to Image" game appears as a playable thumbnail card in the Home game grid and can be opened to the existing route /games/$gameId.

quotes: the game doesnt appear under home in order to start playing it (games created under manager should auto appear as thumbnails to play on home)
it should show the predefined games i create here

acceptanceCriteria: When the app loads, Home shows a thumbnail card for "Match Word to Image" when the backend returns it from getAllGames().
Clicking the "Match Word to Image" card navigates to /games/match-word-to-image and renders the existing Game Launch experience for that game.
If getAllGames() is loading or errors, Home continues to show the existing English loading and error states (no regressions).

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor (all logic in backend/main.mo).
Only add/modify backend/migration.mo if required for state upgrade safety under the project’s conditional migration policy.
Do not edit any frontend files under frontend/src/components/ui or any paths listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Use English for any user-facing text.
nonGoals: Re-enabling in-app creation of new games from the UI (Game Manager must not become a place to create new games).
Adding new gameplay types beyond the existing "Match Word to Image" flow.
Introducing any external database or third-party backend services.
